details.source:
import TaskCard from "./TaskCard"
export interface Task {
  id: string | number
  title: string
  description: string
  priority: string
  completed: boolean
  category?: string
  tags?: string[]
  dueDate?: string | number
}

interface TaskListProps {
  tasks?: Task[]
  countText?: string
  onToggle?: (id: string | number) => void
  onDelete?: (id: string | number) => void
  linkToTaskDetail?: boolean
}


export default function TaskList() {
  return(
  <section id="task-list">
      <TaskCard title="Task One"
       description="First hardcoded task"
        priority="High"
        />
        <TaskCard 
        title="Task Two"
        description="Second hardcoded task"
        priority="Medium"
        />
        <TaskCard 
        title="Task Three"
        description="Third hardcoded task"
        priority="Low"
        />
  </section>
  )
}

strengths: The code is well-organized, with clear separation of concerns between components.
The use of TypeScript interfaces and props typing is excellent, making the code more maintainable and self-documenting.
The component structure is logical, with each component having a single responsibility.
improvements: Some of the component props could be more descriptive. For example, in `TaskCard`, the `taskId` prop is not clearly explained.
In `TaskList`, the hardcoded tasks could be extracted into a separate constant or function to make the code more maintainable.
The CSS styles could be extracted into a separate file to improve code organization and reusability.
aiReviewResults.overall: The code is well-structured, maintainable, and follows good practices. However, there are some minor improvements that can be made to make the code even better.
